VAL-ID-001.notes: NewAgent.tsx imports createAgentIdentity and calls it in the onSuccess callback of the create mutation (line ~120). identity.ts fetches the agent, builds a manifest via buildManifest(), and inserts into agent_identities table with operator_wallet and manifest JSONB fields. Confirmed via live test: creating 'Identity Test Agent' via the form auto-created an agent_identities row with manifest populated (visible in agent detail page ERC-8004 Identity section showing operator_wallet='0x0' and full manifest JSON).

VAL-ID-002.notes: buildManifest() in manifest.ts returns an AgentManifest with all required fields: name (from agent.name), operator_wallet (from param), supported_tools (string[] from ADAPTER_TOOLS map), tech_stacks (hardcoded ['typescript','react','supabase','vite']), compute_constraints (object with max_iterations:100, max_tokens_per_run:200000, budget_usd:10), task_categories (string[] from ROLE_CATEGORIES map). Also includes erc8004_identity field. TypeScript interface in types.ts enforces the shape. Confirmed via live test: manifest JSON displayed on agent detail page contained all required fields.

VAL-ID-003.notes: logExecution() in execution-log.ts inserts into agent_execution_logs table with agent_id, company_id, run_id, log_type, and content (JSONB). Timestamps are auto-generated by Supabase (created_at with default). ExecutionLogType union type in types.ts defines all 6 valid values: 'decision' | 'tool_call' | 'retry' | 'failure' | 'output' | 'safety_check'. The Supabase types.ts confirms agent_execution_logs table schema with id, company_id, agent_id, run_id, log_type, content (Json), and created_at fields.

VAL-ID-004.notes: AgentDetailPage.tsx includes <AgentIdentitySection agentId={id!} /> component. The component (AgentIdentitySection.tsx) uses useAgentIdentity hook to fetch from agent_identities table and displays: Registration Status (Pending/Registered badge), Operator Wallet (truncated address), Chain TX Hash (if registered), and Agent Manifest (formatted JSON). Screenshot shows the newly created 'Identity Test Agent' with full ERC-8004 identity section including manifest content and 'Pending' registration status. CEO agent (demo data) shows empty state: 'No identity registered yet' which is correct since it wasn't created through the standard flow.

VAL-CROSS-002.notes: Created 'Identity Test Agent' (role: engineer, adapter: Codex local) via /agents/new form. After submission: (1) Redirected to agent detail page at /agents/52b2f9e3-333c-4b4e-b2e8-6b50425b554d. (2) Agent visible in agent list at /agents — appears in both sidebar and main list with correct name, role, and status. (3) Agent detail page shows ERC-8004 Identity section with auto-generated manifest containing: name='Identity Test Agent', operator_wallet='0x0' (expected with auth bypass), supported_tools=['code_generation','file_editing','terminal'], task_categories=['code_generation','debugging','code_review','testing'], tech_stacks, compute_constraints, and erc8004_identity. Registration status shows 'Pending'. No console errors observed.

frictions.description: The newly created 'Identity Test Agent' does not appear in the sidebar agent list until a page reload/navigation. After navigating to /agents, it appeared in both sidebar and main list.
frictions.resolution: Navigated to /agents page which refreshed the query cache and displayed the agent correctly.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. VAL-ID-001/002/003 verified through source code review confirming correct data model implementation. VAL-ID-004 verified via agent-browser showing ERC-8004 Identity section with manifest and registration status on agent detail page. VAL-CROSS-002 verified end-to-end: created agent via form, confirmed in list, and verified auto-generated ERC-8004 identity with manifest on detail page.
